steps.title: Set up the proportion
steps.content: The ratio $10$ to $2$ is equivalent to the ratio $40$ to $2k$, so $$\frac{10}{2}=\frac{40}{2k}.$$ The left side simplifies to $5$.

steps.title: Solve for k
steps.content: $$5=\frac{40}{2k}$$ Multiply both sides by $2k$: $$10k=40.$$ Divide by $10$: $$k=4.$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $0.5$: Then $2k=1$, so the ratio $40$ to $2k$ is $40$ to $1$, not $5$. ✗
  • Choice B — $4$: Then $2k=8$, so $\frac{40}{8}=5$, matching $\frac{10}{2}=5$. ✓
  • Choice C — $8$: Then $2k=16$, so the ratio is $40$ to $16$, or $2.5$. ✗
  • Choice D — $80$: Then $2k=160$, so the ratio is $40$ to $160$, or $0.25$. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
